expectations.text: Asks at least 2 clarifying questions before proposing a solution
expectations.evidence: 6 clarifying questions: commit convention, scope (tag-to-tag vs arbitrary range), output destination, grouping, version header, monorepo support. All with default answers.

expectations.text: References actual files or patterns from the cape project codebase
expectations.evidence: References commands/brainstorm.md, existing CHANGELOG.md (Keep a Changelog format), skills/ structure, hooks/hooks.json. Notes 'Cape is a Claude Code plugin' and correctly identifies command pattern.

expectations.text: Proposes at least 2 distinct approaches with pros and cons
expectations.evidence: 2 approaches: (A) Claude Code slash command (recommended) and (B) Shell script hook. Both have pros and cons listed.

expectations.text: Epic contains a Requirements (IMMUTABLE) section with specific, testable statements
expectations.evidence: No epic structure at all. Has 'Implementation outline' and 'Proposed plan' but no Requirements (IMMUTABLE) section. Requirements are implicit in the approach description.

expectations.text: Anti-patterns use 'NO X (reason: Y)' format with reasoning
expectations.evidence: No anti-patterns section. The output mentions what to do but never defines what NOT to do with reasoning.

expectations.text: Creates exactly one first task, not zero or multiple
expectations.evidence: No task structure. Lists 2 files to create (commands/changelog.md and skills/changelog/SKILL.md) but doesn't frame them as a single task with success criteria.
No source code files created. Includes example markdown templates and mapping tables but no executable code.

expectations.text: Includes a Key Decisions table with Question/Answer/Implication columns
expectations.evidence: No Key Decisions table. Questions and defaults are listed individually but never consolidated into a structured table.
